metric_value: 90% of all companies will exit 2006 with SOA activities at least underway
notes: Aberdeen research from 'ESB and SOA Middleware, June 2006'; many large enterprises have >1 year development experience. IBM's positive growth in this area cited as supporting evidence.

metric_value: Mercury acquired Systinet (SOA registry) in January 2006
notes: Mercury gained important SOA management, governance, performance, and development lifecycle capabilities through Systinet acquisition.

metric_value: HP takes giant step closer to IBM; distances from CA and BMC
notes: Aberdeen analysis: HP-Mercury combination is 'highly accretive' to HP OpenView; primarily complementary products with limited overlap; Mercury brings developer-oriented capabilities (application mapping, quality center).

metric_value: bulk of market needs considerable education on IT governance
notes: IT governance not top of mind in survey research especially among SMBs; means regulatory compliance (SOX 404), policy compliance, and IT-business alignment to different buyers.
Culture assimilation of 3,000-person Mercury organization into HP is identified as the 'bigger challenge'; large workforce turnover could delay deliverables and compromise IP.

metric_value: OpenView integration cycle scheduled over next 18 months
notes: HP's planned integration timeline for OpenView-Mercury; customers should review proposed product roadmap and timetable for deliverables.

metric_name: Mainframe involvement in enterprise SOA plans
metric_value: mainframes heavily involved in enterprise-size ($1B+) company SOA plans
notes: Aberdeen most recent survey; HP's lack of committed mainframe strategy limits appeal for large mixed-environment enterprises; zOS can be monitored but deeper mainframe management not HP focus.

metric_value: large enterprises with complex networks, multi-site computing, many IT development projects
notes: HP and Mercury already support wide variety of platforms (Unix, Linux, Windows Server); best suited for large enterprises; small companies (<$50M revenue) unlikely customers — they start with Microsoft.

metric_value: SOA affects entire design-code-test-deploy-maintain application lifecycle
notes: SOA involves new way of developing applications affecting entire lifecycle; SOA operating environment requires new capabilities to monitor and manage SOA infrastructure and services.

metric_value: Aberdeen recommended IT governance as requirement for successful enterprise SOA strategy
notes: From 'The SOA in IT Benchmark Report, December 2005'; Aberdeen had previously recommended IT governance focus for enterprise SOA success.

metric_value: HP likely to remain on acquisitive path; BEA relationship could yield interesting discussions
notes: Kastner speculates HP will continue acquisitions; suggests BEA (which had relationship with Mercury) as potential target. HP did acquire EDS (2008) and 3Com (2010) among others; BEA was acquired by Oracle (2008).

metric_value: IBM showing positive growth and financial performance in SOA area
notes: IBM's SOA market success cited as supporting evidence for broad SOA adoption forecast.
